blocks.content: Now you'll edit the default notification policy to adjust the three timing options. These settings control how long Grafana waits before sending the first grouped notification, how often it sends updates for a group, and how long before it repeats an unchanged alert.
To configure timing, complete the following steps:
On the default policy row, click the **...** button to open the actions menu.
Expand the **Timing options** section to reveal the three timing fields.

Set the **Group wait** value.

Group wait is how long Grafana waits after the first alert in a new group before sending the initial notification. A longer wait batches more alerts into the first message. The default is 30 seconds.

Set the **Group interval** value.

Group interval is the minimum time between notifications for the same group when new alerts are added. The default is 5 minutes.

Set the **Repeat interval** value.

Repeat interval is how long Grafana waits before resending a notification for an alert that hasn't changed. The default is 4 hours. Set this higher to reduce noise from stable alerts.

Click **Update default policy** to save your timing configuration.

The form closes and the policy tree reflects the updated timing values.

Your default policy now has custom timing settings that control notification frequency.

In the next milestone, you'll review what you accomplished.
